title: lomatium junceum
intro: Lomatium junceum, also known as mountain subalpine parsnip, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family native to the mountainous regions of North America. Its roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory issues and infections, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Lomatium junceum are attributed to the presence of isobutyl amide, a compound that has been shown to exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activity, offering potential health benefits such as relief from colds and flu, and improved immune function. Lomatium junceum can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried root, and is often used in herbal supplements to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
